content.body:
I think it is pretty awesome that you are continuing to support the work of the Browser Standards littledan , even after leaving so abruptly. I have a couple comments about your slides:

1. The fact that you named me and suggested that I wrote "almost all" of the standard is not a fair characterization. I designed the approach and wil take the fall for that, but the way you state it is unfair to the contributions of the others, and shows that you don't understand the harmonized standards: It is not my name that will be signed at the end of the process. Also, do you have the explicit permission of Andrew to name him in your screenshot? If so, ok - but just calling that out.
2. The final slide is, I am assuming, leftover from FOSDEM. Please revise it. ETSI's policy on submissions is something you are currently mischaracterizing. You are also not the point of contact anymore, that is why I am sure it is mere oversight.
content.formatted_body:
I think it is pretty awesome that you are continuing to support the work of the Browser Standards littledan , even after leaving so abruptly. I have a couple comments about your slides:

  1. The fact that you named me and suggested that I wrote "almost all" of the standard is not a fair characterization. I designed the approach and wil take the fall for that, but the way you state it is unfair to the contributions of the others, and shows that you don't understand the harmonized standards: It is not my name that will be signed at the end of the process. Also, do you have the explicit permission of Andrew to name him in your screenshot? If so, ok - but just calling that out.
  2. The final slide is, I am assuming, leftover from FOSDEM. Please revise it. ETSI's policy on submissions is something you are currently mischaracterizing. You are also not the point of contact anymore, that is why I am sure it is mere oversight.

content.body: Removed the authorship mention
How do the slides mischaracterize the submission policy?
Changed the contact on the “getting involved” slide to you, but left my contact in the Q/A slide
thanks! and maybe i came off a bit too harsh - i am happy that you are advocating
its just that right now i am moving the drafting process to the final document shape (which is a handcrafted docx) in order to coordinate best with edithelp, laure, and then the commission.
there is ETSI guidance out right now, which states that there is no guarantee at this very late point that OC will be accepted.
that's all
Yeah, I am just encouraging people to file issues, not guaranteeing that they will be addressed
content.formatted_body: Yeah, I am just encouraging people to file issues, not guaranteeing that they will be addressed

content.body: Also, maybe - just maybe - consider asking about how to secure AI
I will mention how things shift to public enquiry at some point, but it’s too much to put on the slides
since that is the one area we punted on
(thinking more about tuesday)
And unrestricted embedded browsers, right?
What about that?
yeah, well, we agreed on friday that embeddable browsers are the product. How they are embedded is PROBABLY a Default concern, not an Important
i mean tuesday you will have a workshop

Oh, good, I agree with that reading of the law, that the embedded browser product is the SFSafariViewController/WKWebView, and the social media app embedding it is a default product, if this is what you mean. That’s separate from our other decision to define requirements for SFSafariViewController and not WKWebView, right?
(Sorry not here to debate)
Dan is live NOW https://www.youtube.com/live/EFp-A7T4c0U?is=Le_6rMT_umLci5nF
